headlines.title: Why Seagate Technology (STX) Is Strengthening Its AI Storage Growth Outlook Through Cloud Demand and Higher-Capacity HDDs
headlines.summary: Seagate Technology Holdings plc (NASDAQ:STX) is one of the best performing AI stocks over the last 3 years, with a 3Y CAGR of 151%. On July 1, Bank of America analyst Wamsi Mohan reiterated a Buy rating and raised his price target to $1,150 from $1,000.

headlines.title: SanDisk Stock Is Trading Like The Memory Cycle Is Dead
headlines.summary: Management went beyond raising the bar, claiming to have rebuilt the entire business model.

headlines.title: Beyond Micron: 2 AI Infrastructure Stocks With Massive Upside Ahead
headlines.summary: Micron's AI-fueled rally has cooled, but Seagate and WDC are riding on strong storage demand, margin expansion and upbeat outlook that may power further gains.

headlines.title: Wells Fargo Just Upgraded Seagate Stock. Here's Why.
headlines.summary: Seagate stock is inching higher after Wells Fargo issued a bullish research note. Here’s how high analyst Aaron Rakers believes STX shares could fly in 2026.

headlines.title: Seagate Stock Rises After Analyst Upgrades To Buy. Western Digital Nabs Price-Target Hike.
headlines.summary: Seagate Technology stock rose Friday, bouncing back from a recent pullback.  "With the recent market pullback and increasing confidence in what we view as a path to plus-$50 earnings-per-share and significant (capital) return capacity ahead, we upgrade STX to overweight with a $1,100 price target," Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers said in a note for clients.  Rakers previously held a neutral equal-weight view for Seagate and a 900 price target.

description: Seagate Technology Holdings plc engages in the provision of data storage technology and infrastructure solutions in Singapore, the United States, the Netherlands, and internationally. The company offers mass capacity storage products, including enterprise nearline hard disk drives (HDDs), enterprise nearline solid state drives (SSDs), enterprise nearline systems, video and image HDDs, and network-attached storage drives. It also offers legacy applications comprising Mission Critical HDDs and SSDs; external storage solutions under the Seagate Ultra Touch, One Touch, Expansion, and Basics product lines, as well as under the LaCie brand name; desktop drives for personal computers and workstation applications; notebook drives traditional notebooks, convertible systems, and external storage applications, DVR HDDs for video streaming applications, and gaming SSDs for gaming rigs. In addition, the company provides Lyve edge-to-cloud mass capacity platform, that includes modular hardware and software to support enterprises' on-premise and cloud storage infrastructure needs. It sells its products primarily to original equipment manufacturers, distributors, and retailers. The company was founded in 1978 and is based in Singapore.
